title: ET Industry Achievers AP and Telangana honours leaders and innovators in Hyderabad
author: Spotlight Wire
short_desc: Hyderabad hosted the ET Industry Achievers – AP & Telangana 2025-26, celebrating leadership and innovation. Actor Boman Irani graced the event as chief guest, honouring 35 individuals and organisations across diverse sectors. The awards recognised significant contributions to industry and society, h
long_desc: The second edition of ET Industry Achievers – AP & Telangana 2025-26 was held on March 18 in Hyderabad at the Novotel HICC, recognising achievements in leadership, innovation and entrepreneurship across sectors. The event brought together business leaders and entrepreneurs from Andhra Pradesh and Telangana.Organised by Optimal Media Solutions, a division of Times Internet Ltd., the awards acknowledged the contributions of individuals and organisations across industries. Pride Motors, Guru’s Universe, and The Reel God were among the partners for the event.Starting on a positive note The event opened with a ceremonial lamp-lighting, followed by the arrival of actor Boman Irani as the chief guest. A dance performance by students from Mantha’s Raagalaya Music & Dance Academy, Secunderabad, followed.Visionaries take the limelightAndhra Pradesh and Telangana share a cultural legacy, yet have distinct identities. They have emerged as powerful engines of progress in India’s economic landscape. Telangana, led by its capital Hyderabad, is a global hub for technology, innovation and life sciences, while Andhra Pradesh is steadily building momentum with a strengthened focus on infrastructure, manufacturing, ports, and emerging industrial corridors.The awards honoured 35 ‘Achievers’ for their outstanding contributions to their respective industries and to society at large.. A wide spectrum of industries was recognised, such as corporate, retail, e-commerce, manufacturing, healthcare and…

title: Dhurandhar 2 delivers a hat-trick for Reliance’s Jio Studios
author: ET Online
short_desc: Jio Studios has achieved a remarkable feat with three consecutive films, Stree 2, Dhurandhar, and Dhurandhar: The Revenge, each surpassing Rs 500 crore net in India. Stree 2 set a new benchmark in 2024, followed by Dhurandhar in 2025 and Dhurandhar: The Revenge in 2026, showcasing a sustained period
long_desc: Jio Studios has recorded a rare run at the box office, delivering three consecutive films that have each crossed the Rs 500 crore net mark in India over the past three years.Stree 2, Dhurandhar and Dhurandhar: The Revenge have all entered the Rs 500 crore club, reflecting a sustained streak of high-performing releases under the studio’s banner.In 2024, Stree 2 set a new benchmark for Hindi cinema. The film crossed Rs 500 crore in India within 18 days, backed by strong word-of-mouth and repeat viewership. It went on to surpass Rs 600 crore within a little over a month, becoming the first Hindi film to reach that milestone in a single language.Read more: Dhurandhar 2: Why even a blockbuster is not bulletproof; PVR INOX's Pramod Arora bets on better theatresThe momentum continued in 2025…

title: First large-scale Russian–Indian joint film production set for release in Moscow
author: Dipanjan Roy Chaudhury
short_desc: Russian producers have partnered with India and the international film industry to create "Persimmon of My Love," a musical comedy entirely shot in India utilizing Bollywood's full production capabilities. This marks the first large-scale Russian-Indian co-production since the Soviet era, set to rel
long_desc: For the first time in modern Russian cinema, Russian producers, in partnership with the Indian and international film industry, have completed a major cross‑border project: the musical comedy Persimmon of My Love.This will be the first Russian wide‑release feature of its kind to be shot entirely in India, using the infrastructure of Indian cities and the full production capabilities of Bollywood. More than 350 Indian and international crew members worked on set every day, not including background actors.In some scenes, up to 1,000 people appeared on screen at once – a scale rarely achieved outside the world’s largest studios and very much in keeping with the visual spectacle traditionally associated with Bollywood.The project is especially significant against the backdrop of expanding…
